growth_records.medium: Defined autotrophic medium (Supplementary Table S4, per ref 23); cysteine-HCl reducing agent

up.method: online micro-GC exhaust gas + mass-flow meter
online micro-GC; no H2 uptake observed

growth_records.cite: Oliveira, Luis et al. Comparison of Syngas-Fermenting Clostridia in Stirred-Tank Bioreactors and the Effects of Varying Syngas Impurities. Microorganisms (2022). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms10040681. [PMC9032146]
growth_records.method: reported (max specific growth rate); doubling time calculated
growth_records.snippet: The highest CDW concentrations were observed with C. ragsdalei (0.56 g L-1)... C. ragsdalei produced considerably more acetate, with a final concentration of 4.61 g L-1... maximal CO uptake rates varied... 16 mmol L-1 h-1 (C. ragsdalei)...
growth_records.notes: Autotrophic reference batch. mu=0.116/h reported; doubling_time = ln2/0.116 = 5.98 h. CO uptake ~16 mmol/L/h VOLUMETRIC max (final CDW 0.56 g/L). Derived total alcohols = alcohol:acetate 0.41 g/g x acetate 4.61 g/L = 1.89 g/L (non-experimental back-calc). Max CO conversion 11.9%. Highest CO uptake/acetate of the three strains.

growth_records.medium: Defined autotrophic medium + 0.1 g/L H2S (added as thioacetamide, TAA) before inoculation

up.method: online micro-GC exhaust gas + mass-flow meter

sec.units: g/L (increased vs reference; product shifted from acetate to alcohol; higher biomass-related yield)

growth_records.cite: Oliveira, Luis et al. Comparison of Syngas-Fermenting Clostridia in Stirred-Tank Bioreactors and the Effects of Varying Syngas Impurities. Microorganisms (2022). DOI:10.3390/microorganisms10040681. [PMC9032146]
growth_records.method: not reported for this condition (final CDW +34% vs reference)
growth_records.snippet: The addition of 0.1 g L-1 H2S increased the final cell dry weight concentration of C. ragsdalei by 34%. Higher CO uptake rates were also observed, with maximal CO uptake rates reaching approximately 21 mmol L-1 h-1. Product formation shifte
growth_records.notes: Only stimulatory impurity condition in the study. CO uptake ~21 mmol/L/h VOLUMETRIC (up from ~16 in reference). Derived final CDW = reference 0.56 g/L x 1.34 = 0.75 g/L. mu not separately reported for this condition. Absolute ethanol/2,3-BDO titers only shown in Figure 3.
